title: Statistical permutation quantifiers in the classical transition of conservative-dissipative systems
summary: We study the behavior of a nonlinear semiclassical system using Shannon entropy and two approaches to statistical complexity. These systems involve the interaction between classical variables (representing the environment) and quantum ones. Both conservative and dissipative regimes are explored. To calculate the information metrics, probability distributions are derived from the temporal evolution via the Bandt-Pompe permutation method. Additionally, we describe the classical limit in terms of a motion invariant linked to the uncertainty principle. Our analysis reveals three distinct regions, including a mesoscopic one, along with other notable findings.
